data.title: Birkenstock Beats Growth Targets on Strong Holiday Quarter, Invests in New Production Capacity
data.description:
Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Birkenstock Holding plc ( (BIRK) ) has issued an announcement.

On February 12, 2026, Birkenstock reported fiscal first-quarter 2026 results for the period ended December 31, 2025, with revenue rising 11% to €402 million on a reported basis and 18% in constant currency, outpacing its full-year growth target. Strong holiday demand across clogs, shearling styles and closed-toe footwear drove double-digit constant-currency gains in all regions, led by B2B channels and supported by nine new company-owned stores, bringing its global retail network to 106 locations.

Despite a 460-basis-point drop in gross margin to 55.7% due to adverse currency moves, higher U.S. tariffs and channel mix effects, net profit jumped 151% to €51 million and earnings per share rose to €0.27, while adjusted EBITDA grew 4% with margin compression tied mainly to FX and tariffs. The company continued to invest for growth, allocating about €38 million in first-quarter capital expenditures—including €18 million for a new production site in Wittichenau—while ending the quarter with €229 million in cash and net leverage of 1.7x, underscoring both strong demand and a strategy of expanding capacity to meet its long-term ambitions.

The most recent analyst rating on (BIRK) stock is a Buy with a $42.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Birkenstock Holding plc stock, see the BIRK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BIRK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BIRK is a Outperform.

Birkenstock’s strong financial performance and positive earnings call are the primary drivers of its stock score. While technical indicators suggest some caution, the company’s robust growth and expansion plans support a favorable outlook. Valuation is moderate, reflecting a balanced risk-reward profile.

To see Spark’s full report on BIRK stock, click here.

More about Birkenstock Holding plc

Birkenstock Holding plc is the parent of the Birkenstock Group, a global footwear and lifestyle brand rooted in a family shoemaking tradition dating back to 1774. Best known as the inventor of the footbed, the company focuses on products that promote proper foot health, spanning footwear, sleep systems and natural cosmetics across entry-level to luxury price points.

Average Trading Volume: 2,631,554

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $7.39B

For an in-depth examination of BIRK stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

Noah Holdings ( (NOAH) ) has shared an update.

Noah Holdings filed a Form 6-K in February 2026 disclosing a Next Day Disclosure Return for its Hong Kong-listed arm, confirming that its ordinary share count on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange remained unchanged at 335,258,287 as of February 10, 2026. The filing also detailed that, between December 23, 2025 and January 28, 2026, the company steadily repurchased American depositary shares on the New York Stock Exchange for cancellation under a June 12, 2025 buyback mandate, signaling ongoing capital management efforts aimed at reducing free float and potentially enhancing shareholder value without altering the current issued share base in Hong Kong.

data.title: InMed Highlights Q2 Results Amid Cash, Regulatory Pressures
data.description:
Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

InMed Pharmaceuticals ( (INM) ) has provided an update.

On February 11, 2026, InMed reported second-quarter fiscal 2026 results for the period ended December 31, 2025, highlighting progress in its neurology and ophthalmology programs while managing a tightening cash position. The company completed key pharmacokinetic studies for Alzheimer’s candidate INM-901, showing robust oral bioavailability with no adverse neurological effects in large animals, and advanced preclinical work on INM-089 for dry AMD, as it prepares for pre-IND meetings and GLP-enabling studies supporting future IND submissions.

Financially, BayMedica revenue fell 26% year over year to $0.8 million on weaker demand linked to pending U.S. legislative changes, while R&D expenses declined to $0.6 million and G&A held roughly flat at $1.6 million. Cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments dropped to $7.0 million from $11.1 million at June 30, 2025, with management expecting liquidity to last into the fourth quarter of calendar 2026, though potential implementation of H.R. 5371 could force BayMedica inventory write-offs, strategic divestiture or shutdown, posing a material risk to future operations and financial performance.

The most recent analyst rating on (INM) stock is a Sell with a $0.88 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on InMed Pharmaceuticals stock, see the INM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on INM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, INM is a Neutral.

The score is primarily pressured by weak financial performance—persistent losses, contracting revenue, and ongoing cash burn—followed by bearish technical signals with the stock trading below major moving averages. Valuation offers limited support due to negative earnings and no dividend data, while corporate events are mixed with a meaningful regulatory headwind offset by R&D progress and added financing flexibility.

To see Spark’s full report on INM stock, click here.

More about InMed Pharmaceuticals

InMed Pharmaceuticals Inc., based in Vancouver and listed on Nasdaq as INM, is a pharmaceutical drug development company focused on proprietary small-molecule therapies for diseases with high unmet medical need. Its pipeline includes INM-901 for Alzheimer’s disease targeting neuroinflammation and INM-089 for dry age-related macular degeneration, alongside a commercial rare cannabinoid business operated through its BayMedica subsidiary.

Average Trading Volume: 370,598

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $2.8M

Learn more about INM stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Zillow Group Inc Class C: Credit Facility Covenants and Rising Rates Threaten Liquidity and Financial Flexibility
data.description:
Zillow Group Inc Class C (Z) has disclosed a new risk, in the Debt & Financing category.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Zillow Group Inc Class C faces constraints from its Revolving Credit Facility, whose covenants limit its ability to incur additional debt, pledge assets, or pursue certain investments and distributions. Any breach could trigger default, allowing lenders to cut access to liquidity, accelerate repayment, or seize collateral, pressuring operations and its stock price.

Because borrowings under the facility bear interest at variable rates, Zillow Group Inc Class C is also exposed to interest rate volatility, which can increase debt service costs even if principal balances remain unchanged. This combination of restrictive covenants and rate sensitivity heightens the company’s refinancing risk and may impair its long‑term financial flexibility.

The average Z stock price target is $80.20, implying 75.65% upside potential.

To learn more about Zillow Group Inc Class C’s risk factors, click here.

data.title: Arrow Electronics Faces Rising Legal, Regulatory, and IP Risks That Threaten Profitability and Competitive Position
data.description:
Arrow Electronics, Inc. (ARW) has disclosed a new risk, in the Regulation category.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Arrow Electronics, Inc. faces heightened exposure to complex and evolving legal and regulatory frameworks across the U.S. and other jurisdictions, increasing the risk of claims, investigations, and litigation. Such matters can be unpredictable and costly, potentially diverting management resources, damaging its reputation, and resulting in material penalties or settlements that weigh on financial performance.

Because Arrow relies heavily on third‑party and internally developed intellectual property, it remains vulnerable to infringement and indemnification claims, including from non‑practicing entities seeking licensing revenue. Adverse outcomes could force it to halt product sales, secure expensive licenses on unfavorable terms, or absorb substantial damage awards, all of which could impair its competitive position and operating results.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tapestry has seen a notable insider transaction as Todd Kahn, CEO and Brand President of Coach, sold 39,815 shares of Tapestry stock in a single move. The sale, totaling $6,025,599, highlights a multi-million-dollar shift in the executive’s holdings and is likely to draw attention from investors watching insider activity at the company.

Recent Updates on TPR stock

Tapestry shares reacted to a blowout Q2 in which revenue and EPS beat expectations, margins expanded despite tariff headwinds, and management raised full‑year revenue, EPS, and margin guidance while boosting cash‑return plans. Analysts’ recent target moves and rating changes largely reflect this strong execution and upgraded outlook, tempered by a more balanced risk/reward after the stock’s run, ongoing tariff pressures, and the strategic but margin‑dilutive reset at Kate Spade.

Spark’s Take on TPR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TPR is a Neutral.

The score is driven by strong cash-flow generation and an upbeat earnings call with raised guidance and shareholder returns, supported by clear upward price momentum. These positives are tempered by high balance-sheet leverage and a notably elevated valuation (high P/E), which increase downside risk if margin pressure, tariffs, or the Kate Spade turnaround worsen.

data.title: Insider Shake-Up: Virtus Investment Partners Director Executes Major Stock Sale
data.description:
New insider activity at Virtus Investment Partners ( (VRTS) ) has taken place on February 11, 2026.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Director Susan Fleming has recently trimmed her stake in Virtus Investment Partners, selling 3,322 shares of the company’s stock in a transaction valued at $472,760. This insider move highlights notable trading activity within the firm’s leadership ranks, drawing attention from investors watching Virtus Investment Partners’ ownership changes and potential shifts in executive confidence.

Recent Updates on VRTS stock

Virtus Investment Partners’ stock reacted to Q4 results showing solid revenue but pressured earnings as large net outflows and equity style headwinds cut AUM, partly offset by strong ETF inflows and M&A-driven diversification into private markets. Analysts trimmed price targets mainly because of the persistently challenging flow profile, ongoing pressure in U.S. retail and institutional equity strategies, and the earnings sensitivity to further outflows despite balance-sheet strength and ETF growth.

Spark’s Take on VRTS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VRTS is a Neutral.

Virtus Investment Partners shows a solid financial position with strong profitability and cash flow improvements. However, high leverage and revenue decline pose risks. The stock’s low valuation and high dividend yield are attractive, while recent corporate events and strategic acquisitions provide a positive outlook. Technical indicators suggest neutral momentum, and the earnings call reflects a balanced view of strengths and challenges.
